LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO SEP-02-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER HEARING FOR MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION TO DEPARTMENT 302; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. AIRBNB, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER THE HEARING FOR ITS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION TO DEPARTMENT 302 IS GRANTED. THE COURT GRANTS THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS. DEFENDANT'S (AIRBNB, INC.) MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE CORRECTION THAT MORGAN MUSSELWHITE AND LUKE MUSSELWHITE WILL BE IDENTIFIED AS USERS AND SUBJECT TO THE RULING APPLYING TO THE USERS AND ONLY 3 OF THE PLAINTIFFS WILL BE DEEMED AS GUESTS AND BE EXCLUDED FROM THE REQUIREMENT OF ARBITRATION. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Continued To Sep-30-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Matt Kita, Esq. Elise Sanguinetti, Esq. 925-689-7070 Zwicker & Associates for Plaintiff.
Karen L. Prodromo, Esq. 925-478-3270 Lewis Brisbois for Defendant.

ARGUED. Ms. Prodromo contests and argues; Perhaps plaintiffs were miscategorized. Ms. Kita responds to the issue.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Defendant's (AIRBNB, INC.) motion to compel arbitration and stay is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. (see order for full details)

The court adopts the tentative ruling with the correction that Morgan Musselwhite and Luke Musselwhite will be identified as users and subject to the ruling applying to the users and only 3 of the plaintiffs will be deemed as guests and be excluded from the requirement of arbitration.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Continued To Nov-26-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-15-2025 Continued To Oct-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Hearing For Motion To Compel Arbitration To Department 302
Off Calendar Sep-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Matthew Kita, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Team LLP (tel: 510-629-4877) for the Plaintiffs.
Atty. Karen Prodromo, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (tel: 925-357-3456) for Defendant Airbnb, Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'s Motion To Transfer Hearing For Motion To Compel Arbitration To Department 302.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Airbnb, Inc.'s motion to transfer the hearing for its motion to compel arbitration to Department 302 is granted. The court grants the request for judicial notice.

Three cases have been filed arising out of the same incident: the present case, Lacourly, et al. v. Airbnb, Inc., et al., San Francisco County Superior Court Case No. CGC24-619210, and Tharp, et al. v. Airbnb, Inc., et al., San Francisco County Superior Court Case No. CGC-25-624502. Motions to compel arbitration brought by Airbnb are set for hearing in this case (for October 30, 2025) and in Tharp (for October 8, 2025). Lacourly and Tharp are earlier filed cases with even case numbers. There are common questions of fact and law presented by the motions to compel arbitration in the Tharp and Marshall cases. The court finds that it will promote the ends of justice and conserve judicial resources to transfer the motion to compel arbitration in this case to Department 302.

The court vacates the October 30 hearing presently set for the motion to compel arbitration in this case and resets the motion for hearing on October 8 to join the Tharp matter.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
